evidence: `broadcast.Initialize` defines an extension point but it is never used (`_ = options` after `func Initialize(options ...broadcastRuntimeOption) error` in `goseg/broadcast/broadcast.go`).

`orchestration/runtime_seams.go` introduces runtime aliases and constructors (`wireguardRuntime`, `netdataRuntime`, `minioRuntime`, `urbitRuntime`) that mostly forward to `newDockerRuntime()` without adding service-specific behavior.

The service wrappers in `goseg/docker/orchestration/wireguard.go` and `goseg/docker/orchestration/netdata.go` follow the same pattern: exported call -> `*_forRuntime(newXRuntime())`, while the `forRuntime` function is mostly a direct implementation wrapper.

findings.summary: The broadcast runtime option API is a dead abstraction: option hooks are accepted but never applied, creating misleading extension points and silent configuration bypass.
evidence: `Initialize` is declared as `func Initialize(options ...broadcastRuntimeOption) error` but only contains `_ = options`, never executing or storing any injected option.
There is no follow-up call path that applies or validates options before `bootstrapBroadcastState`, so callers cannot influence bootstrap behavior through this seam.
Startup always invokes `broadcast.Initialize()` directly with no options, matching the non-usage pattern.
findings.suggestion: Either remove the unused `broadcastRuntimeOption` extension hook or implement it by applying each option to configure init behavior in a single, explicit place inside `Initialize` (for example, injecting alternate runtimes, config sources, or initialization policies). Then add unit tests that assert non-default options alter initialization.

findings.summary: Service-specific runtime adapter layers are overly verbose and mostly pass-through, adding indirection without introducing meaningful service-specific policy.
evidence: `runtime_seams.go` defines per-service aliases (`type wireguardRuntime dockerRuntime`, `type netdataRuntime dockerRuntime`, etc.) and `newWireguardRuntime/newNetdataRuntime/newMinIORuntime/newUrbitRuntime` simply return `newDockerRuntime()`.

`wireguard.go` and `netdata.go` export thin wrappers (`LoadWireguard` -> `loadWireguardForRuntime(newWireguardRuntime())`, `LoadNetdata` -> `loadNetdata(newNetdataRuntime())`) with almost identical indirection style.

`config/wireguard.go` follows the same pattern (`GetWgConf`/`UpdateWGConf` -> `getWgConf(newWireguardRuntime())` / `updateWGConf(newWireguardRuntime())`) and does not add policy between caller and implementation.
This same style appears in helper functions like `WriteWgConf`/`WriteNDConf` and file-copy wrappers, increasing tracing cost and duplication for little behavioral separation.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate these wrappers into a single typed runtime factory/use-case seam (for example, a parameterized container-runtime constructor with service-specific op sets) and remove redundant service aliases unless they encapsulate distinct policy; keep one wrapper path per operation.
